metadata.title: Cross-system contagion & interconnection map - the transmission channels that link the otherwise-siloed bubbles (crypto<->Treasury<->banks<->AI marks; yen carry<->AI core; chip<->private credit; China leverage; the Fed circuit-breaker)
metadata.frame: The other blocks each map one subsystem; this block draws the EDGES BETWEEN them - the transmission channels along which a shock in one bubble propagates to the others. All edges here are STRUCTURAL OVERLAY (relationship / regulatory_legal): directional transmission links, not new money-flows, so they enrich the bubble map and connector analysis but are EXCLUDED from the SCC / Z3 / TLA+ proofs (which run on the financial layer only). Each channel is evidence-graded; the contagion is a documented mechanism, the firing of it is contingent. Also canonicalizes three figures that appear inconsistently across blocks, and states the highest-value open questions plainly.
metadata.relevance: Interleaves [[macro-stablecoin-treasury-rail]] + [[macro-stablecoin-failures-manipulation]] + [[macro-bank-htm-marks]] + [[spec-unwind-timing]] (the crypto->Treasury->bank cascade); [[macro-carry-trades]] + [[fin-microsoft-openai]] (yen-carry into the AI core via SoftBank); [[fin-google-amazon-anthropic-meta]] + [[macro-cre-privatecredit]] (chip<->private-credit financing); [[geopolitics-chip-chokepoint-war]] + [[spec-rare-earth-statecraft]] + [[geopolitics-defense-industrial-base]] (China leverage); and adds the Fed as the circuit-breaker node absent elsewhere.
metadata.sources: Synthesis of the cited blocks above plus primary reconciliations (NVIDIA/OpenAI: nvidianews + Fortune Feb-2026; Oracle RPO: Oracle Q1-FY26 release 2025-09-09; Anthropic Series G: anthropic.com + TechCrunch 2026-02-12). Full URLs in the rendered .md.

channel_crypto_treasury_bank.summary: The most important missing cascade: a stablecoin run forces a Treasury-bill fire-sale, which spikes yields, which reopens bank held-to-maturity losses, which contracts credit into the AI buildout. Crypto, Treasuries, banks, and AI capex are one system, not four.
channel_crypto_treasury_bank.mechanism: Stablecoins (Tether ~$140B+, USDC) hold their reserves overwhelmingly in T-bills. A loss of peg / redemption run forces fire-sale of T-bills -> yields spike at the front end -> the move up in rates deepens banks' unrealized HTM losses (already ~$300B+ and re-widening) -> stressed banks pull credit / raise equity -> the marginal lender to the AI/data-center buildout retrenches, pressuring the very marks ([[fin-ai-depreciation-debttrap]]) the core depends on. The chain is directional and well-evidenced as a mechanism; whether it fires depends on a peg break (the trigger).
channel_crypto_treasury_bank.grade: fact (each link individually documented) + contingent (the cascade firing)

channel_yen_carry_ai_core.summary: The yen carry trade is the hidden funding leg under the AI buildout: cheap yen funds crowded global longs and SoftBank's capital, so a BOJ-driven carry unwind transmits straight into the AI core.
channel_yen_carry_ai_core.mechanism: Japan's near-zero rates made the yen the world's funding currency; as JGB yields escape yield-curve control (0% -> ~2.66% in 2026), the carry cost rises and crowded carry-funded longs unwind ([[macro-carry-trades]]). SoftBank - a major OpenAI/Stargate financier - is yen-funded and yen-refinancing-exposed; a carry unwind raises its cost of capital and refinancing risk exactly as its AI commitments peak. So a Tokyo rates move is an AI-core funding shock.
channel_yen_carry_ai_core.grade: fact (carry mechanism; SoftBank yen exposure) + contingent (unwind magnitude/timing)

channel_chip_private_credit.summary: Chip vendors are now also infrastructure financiers, fusing the semiconductor and private-credit bubbles: Broadcom's XPU compute-financing platform (with Apollo/Blackstone) and Meta's Blue Owl JV route private credit into the same buildout the chips serve.
channel_chip_private_credit.mechanism: Broadcom (custom XPUs for Google/Meta/OpenAI/Anthropic + two unnamed customers, analyst-flagged Apple/ByteDance) launched a ~$35B+ XPU compute-financing platform with Apollo/Blackstone; Meta funds Hyperion via a Blue Owl SPV. Private-credit capital ([[macro-cre-privatecredit]] / [[macro-private-credit-marks]]) thus finances the data centers that buy the chips - a second circular structure beside the equity loop, and a second place a private-credit mark-down would bite.
channel_chip_private_credit.grade: fact (the platforms) + labeled interpretation (the circularity read)

channel_china_leverage.summary: With the fragmented China nodes merged, one China actor now visibly holds multiple chokepoints over the Western AI/defense stack simultaneously - rare earths, chip inputs, undersea-cable and comms exposure - which is the point: the leverage is concentrated, not diffuse.
channel_china_leverage.mechanism: The same China node gates rare-earth/magnet supply to defense primes and the AI hardware chain ([[spec-rare-earth-statecraft]] / [[macro-critical-minerals]]), is the prime suspect in Baltic/Taiwan undersea-cable cuts and the Royal Mint Court comms-siting exposure ([[geopolitics-cables-space-layer]]), and runs the export-licensing 'faucet' on/off ([[geopolitics-chip-chokepoint-war]]). Merging the node makes the single-actor concentration legible across the map.
channel_china_leverage.grade: fact (the individual chokepoints) + labeled interpretation (concentration as deliberate leverage)

channel_fed_circuit_breaker.summary: The Fed is the missing node in the unwind scenarios: every cascade above can be interrupted (or amplified) by Fed policy - rate cuts, QE restart, or an emergency facility like a reopened BTFP - so any unwind thesis that assumes policy inaction is incomplete.
channel_fed_circuit_breaker.mechanism: If the crypto->Treasury->bank cascade fires, the Fed's playbook is a backstop: a BTFP-style facility lets banks borrow against par (suppressing the HTM-forced-sale spiral), and QE / rate cuts cap the yield spike. This does not prevent the loss - it socializes and delays it - but it changes the timing and form of any unwind. The trigger panel ([[spec-unwind-timing]]) should be read against this reaction function.
channel_fed_circuit_breaker.grade: fact (the tools exist + precedent: BTFP 2023) + contingent (whether/when deployed)

fed_reaction_function.summary: #57 - a FORMAL decision-tree for the circuit-breaker node: given which cascade fires, what the Fed does, in what order, gated by what constraint. Tools + precedents are fact; the trigger thresholds and branch selection are a labeled model. The binding constraint is the inflation gate - this is the Z3 'no single rate fits the targets' result (macro-fed-trap-regional) rendered as a tree.

decision_tree.root: A shock fires in one channel (crypto->Treasury, yen-carry->core, chip<->private-credit, China faucet). The Fed observes the SYMPTOM (which market is dysfunctional) and reaches for the matching tool - escalating from cheapest/least-signaling to most.

branches.trigger: B1 FUNDING-MARKET STRESS - repo rate / front-end T-bill yield spikes (a stablecoin run dumps bills, or a basis-trade unwind clogs repo)
branches.tool_order: Standing Repo Facility (SRF) first -> expand counterparties/size -> if it persists, outright bill purchases
branches.threshold: SOFR-IORB spread blows out / repo prints far above the SRF rate (cf. Sep-2019 repo spike)

branches.trigger: B2 BANK SOLVENCY / DEPOSIT RUN - a yield spike reopens HTM losses (~$300B+); an uninsured-deposit run forces HTM realization (SVB mode)
branches.tool_order: discount window -> a BTFP-style facility (borrow against par, not market value) -> FDIC systemic-risk exception on deposits
branches.threshold: a named bank fails / uninsured-deposit outflows accelerate
branches.cost: SOCIALIZES the mark - suppresses the forced-sale spiral but does not erase the loss; moral hazard

branches.trigger: B3 TREASURY-MARKET DYSFUNCTION - dealer balance sheets can't absorb the fire-sale; bid-ask/illiquidity breaks
branches.tool_order: outright Treasury purchases (QE restart) -> SLR/eSLR relief to free dealer capacity (ties macro-history-dereg-manipulation #77)
branches.threshold: Treasury-market liquidity metrics break / a failed auction tail
branches.precedent: Mar-2020 'dash for cash' QE + 2020 SLR exclusion
branches.cost: balance-sheet expansion; the SLR lever is already half-pulled (eSLR cut Nov-2025)

branches.trigger: B4 BROAD CREDIT CONTRACTION / RECESSION - stressed banks pull credit to the AI/data-center buildout; unemployment rises
branches.tool_order: rate CUTS (the conventional lever) -> forward guidance
branches.threshold: labor-market deterioration past the Fed's tolerance (U-3 break; cf. macro-gig-labor hidden slack)
branches.cost: the slowest-acting; collides with the inflation gate below

decision_tree.the_inflation_gate: THE BINDING CONSTRAINT across every branch: if measured inflation is still above target (CPI ~4.2%, cf. macro-official-data-integrity), easing to backstop the cascade reignites inflation, while holding to fight inflation lets the cascade run. The Fed cannot simultaneously (a) cap the yield spike, (b) protect bank capital, and (c) hold inflation down with one rate - exactly the Z3 UNSAT result in macro-fed-trap-regional ('no single rate fits the targets'). So the reaction function is ASYMMETRIC and inflation-gated: liquidity tools (B1-B3, which expand the balance sheet against collateral) fire FAST because they can be framed as 'plumbing, not stimulus'; the rate-cut lever (B4) is gated by inflation and fires SLOW.
decision_tree.terminal_insight: The Fed does not PREVENT the loss in any branch - it socializes and DELAYS it (B2/B3) or trades inflation for it (B4). So the unwind's TIMING and FORM are set by which constraint binds first: a plumbing break (fast backstop, inflationary) vs a solvency break (par-lending, moral hazard) vs a labor break (rate cuts, gated). Read the trigger panel (spec-unwind-timing) against this tree: any unwind thesis assuming policy INACTION is incomplete, and any assuming a clean rescue ignores the inflation gate.

fed_reaction_function.grade: Fed tools + precedents (SRF 2019, BTFP 2023, QE/SLR 2020) FACT; trigger thresholds + branch model + the inflation-gate framing LABELED (grounded in the macro-fed-trap Z3 result)

reconciliations.summary: Three figures appear in more than one form across blocks; the canonical values, for consistency.

items.canonical: The '$100B' was a milestone-linked LETTER OF INTENT (22 Sep 2025), tied to deploying 10GW; NVIDIA's Huang later called it 'never a commitment.' The CLOSED figure is $30B direct equity, as part of OpenAI's ~$110B round at a $730B pre-money valuation (27 Feb 2026; SoftBank +$30B, Amazon +$50B). Cite $30B as closed; treat $100B as a lapsed LOI. Canonical in [[fin-nvidia-openai]].

$455B remaining performance obligations at end of Q1 FY2026 (quarter ended 31 Aug 2025; reported 9 Sep 2025), up +359% YEAR-OVER-YEAR (vs ~$99B a year earlier), driven by four multi-billion contracts across three customers (OpenAI/xAI/Meta/NVIDIA/AMD). The $317B figure is the PRIOR QUARTER (Q4 FY2025, ended 31 May 2025) - so $317B->$455B is the QoQ step and +359% is the YoY rate; not a discrepancy once the periods are labeled.

Closed 12 Feb 2026 at $350B PRE-money / $380B POST-money; the round expanded from a ~$20B target to $30B final on demand (led by GIC and Coatue). So '$350B' and '$380B' are pre vs post, and '$10-30B' is target vs final - both pairs are the same event, not conflicting rounds.

open_questions.summary: The highest-value questions the corpus cannot yet answer from public sources, with what would resolve each. These are genuine investigative gaps, stated so they are not mistaken for settled facts.

items.q: What are the termination/notice terms on the cancelable AI-compute deals (SpaceX<->Google $920M/mo; OpenAI<->Oracle/CoreWeave)?
items.resolves_with: the contracts or 10-K/S-1 commitment disclosures; determines how fast the robust SCC can shed nodes.

items.q: What performance milestones gate Google's contingent $30B in Anthropic (of the headline $40B)?
items.resolves_with: a 13-D/round disclosure; determines whether a miss collapses the second funding ring.

items.q: What is CoreWeave's debt maturity schedule (~$21.4B; $6.7B current), and which bullets hit in 2026-27 as the Microsoft revenue share falls below 50%?
Are Broadcom's two unnamed XPU customers Apple and/or ByteDance (a US export-control question)?
items.resolves_with: Broadcom earnings disclosure or Apple/ByteDance capex confirmation.

items.q: How large is SoftBank's yen-denominated refinancing need across 2026-27 relative to its AI commitments?
items.resolves_with: SoftBank debt schedule; sizes the carry-unwind transmission.

non_claims: These transmission edges are NOT new money-flows and do NOT enter the formal proofs - they are structural-overlay channels for reading propagation.
It is NOT asserted that any cascade WILL fire - each is a documented mechanism whose trigger is contingent (and, for the Fed channel, potentially interrupted).
The China-concentration read is leverage/exposure, not an assertion of intent to use every chokepoint.
The reconciliations correct labeling/period/stage confusion; they do not allege error by any source.

edges.raw_instrument: stablecoin reserves are T-bill-concentrated; a run forces fire-sale (transmission channel)
edges.note: OVERLAY transmission: crypto->Treasury; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: ~$140B+ reserves heavily in T-bills; single-issuer concentration in the Treasury bid
a yield spike deepens bank HTM unrealized losses (~$300B+ and re-widening) - mark-reversal channel
edges.note: OVERLAY transmission: Treasury->banks; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: stressed banks contract credit to the AI/data-center buildout (the marginal lender retrenches)
edges.note: OVERLAY transmission: banks->AI credit; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: yen-carry-funded capital into the AI buildout - a Tokyo rates move is an AI-core funding shock
edges.note: OVERLAY: carry->core funding channel; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: SoftBank yen-refinancing exposure sits under its OpenAI commitments (carry-unwind transmission)
edges.note: OVERLAY: carry exposure; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: analyst-flagged as one of Broadcom's two unnamed custom-XPU customers (unconfirmed) - links Apple capex to the XPU stack
edges.note: OVERLAY: unconfirmed customer link; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: circuit-breaker: a BTFP-style facility lets banks borrow at par, suppressing the HTM forced-sale spiral (precedent: 2023)
edges.note: OVERLAY: Fed circuit-breaker; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: circuit-breaker: QE / rate cuts cap a yield spike (socializes and delays the loss, changing unwind timing)
edges.note: OVERLAY: Fed circuit-breaker; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: rare-earth/magnet + antimony chokepoint over the defense-industrial base (export-licensing faucet)
edges.note: OVERLAY: China leverage over defense; excluded from proofs

limits: A TRANSMISSION-CHANNEL overlay that interleaves the otherwise-siloed subsystem blocks - it adds directional, structural edges (no cycles, excluded from the financial SCC and the Z3/TLA+ proofs) plus a reconciliations index and a plainly-stated open-questions list. Each channel's individual links are documented facts; the cascade firing is contingent and, for the Fed channel, potentially interrupted. No new money-flows are asserted. source: see the cited blocks and the rendered .md for full URLs.
